info.name: text-insert
info.text: The theory is strong, but the foundation is weak.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: The theory is strong, but the foundation is not very stable.

info.name: text-insert
info.text: He's trying to build a strong case for his theory.

info.text: He's trying to build a strong floor to be the groundwork for his theory.

info.name: text-insert
info.text: His theory collapsed under the weight of evidence.

info.text: His theory collapsed like a pile of bricks under the weight of external scrutiny.

info.name: text-insert
info.text: That's a weak foundation for your argument.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: It's not good to have a weak foundation for your argument.

info.name: text-insert
info.text: His theory was quickly demolished by the evidence.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: A bad theory is quickly demolished by evidence.

info.text: A good theory should be incredibly well-constructed.

info.name: text-insert
info.text: That argument is weak - it's just a building of straw.

info.text: His theory fell apart under close inspection.
